reasoning: Rafał Trzaskowski is the strong frontrunner for the 2025 Polish presidential election, leading comfortably in all recent First Round polls (averaging 30-38%) ahead of his closest competitor Karol Nawrocki (averaging 24-26%). He is also heavily favored in hypothetical Second Round run-offs, where almost all polls show him defeating Nawrocki or far-right candidate Sławomir Mentzen by a comfortable margin (often in the range of 55-45 or 60-40 when excluding undecideds). Additionally, Trzaskowski benefits from the structural advantage of the liberal-left coalition that won a parliamentary majority in 2023. At the time of a recently known Polymarket estimate (April 17), the market priced his victory at 75%. While his lead is commanding, ~25% is an appropriate risk discount for the possibility of a right-wing turnout surge in the run-off (similar to his narrow 2020 loss against Andrzej Duda) or faltering mobilization among voters from competing left-wing candidates.
